# FM cockpit agent detail view: drill-in panes with freshness-labeled claims

## Context

T4.14 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — click an agent card → the resident's detail: thought-stream, current lane, repo activity, PRs. The SSOT's caption names it ("Click an agent → its thought-stream, lane, repo, and PRs"). Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561).

## The Problem

The detail view is where the cockpit's claims become CHECKABLE — and therefore where stale data lies hardest. A lane line that is 40 minutes old rendered as current is exactly the class the #13444 graduation's freshness ledger forbids: every mutable claim carries its observation time and stale renders AS stale. No surface needs that discipline more than the drill-in.

## The Architectural Reality

- Composes the card contract (#14605 fields at expanded grain) + #14593/#14594 primitives; namespace per #14577.
- Panes bind the same Lane-C feeds (#14572/#14573) plus per-agent memory-surface reads where contracts exist; **every pane header renders `observedAt` + freshness state** (the ADR 0032 §2.2.1 ledger fields at UI grain).
- The ADR 0032 render rules govern the identity header: trail-first (deeds/lanes), evolution-rate slot reserved (consumes the #14565 instruments when they land), no role-typing anywhere.
- Pop-out (T4.15) is a separate leaf — this view must render shell-agnostic (dev-server now, OS-window host later).

## The Fix

The per-view triplet: (1) AgentDetail view (identity header per ADR 0032 · lane pane · activity pane · PRs pane, each freshness-labeled); (2) unit specs — pane freshness rendering (fresh/stale/lost fixtures), header render rules, unknown-pane-data degrade; (3) NL-verifiable drill from a live card.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every pane renders `observedAt` + freshness state; a stale fixture renders visibly stale (never silently current).
- [ ] Identity header conforms to the #14605 contract + ADR 0032 (name-as-display-state · engine-as-metadata · no role fields).
- [ ] Shell-agnostic mount (no Electron dependency in the view).
- [ ] Unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verifiable drill-in from the grid.

## Out of Scope

Pop-out to OS window (T4.15) · drill e2e round-trip (T4.16) · thought-stream SOURCE contracts (Lane-C/Memory-Core seams — consumed as they exist, panes degrade honestly where they don't).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · blocked-by #14605/#14598 · consumes #14572/#14573 · ADR 0032 (PR #14583) · #13444 freshness carry-forward · D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: fold-window fresh (newest #14607); no equivalent. A2A sweep: clean. Structure-map gate: N/A — app-layer view.

Systemic token-level findings (faint fails AA on all panels for small text; the is-lost alert chip is 4.26:1 AA-large-only) route to design authority under #14619."
- 2026-07-12T03:33:52Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `5807627` - "fix(agentos): AgentDetail liveness + html-safe body — address exact-head review (#14608)

Addresses @neo-gpt-emmy's exact-head CHANGES_REQUESTED on the drill-in view:

- Reactive to record MUTATION (not just re-seat): the cockpit now routes the roster store's recordChange to the open AgentDetail (onDetailRecordChange, mirroring how the grid routes to cards), so a re-poll mutating the inspected agent re-renders in place.
- Reactive to WALL-CLOCK: startFreshnessAging re-classifies the panes every freshnessRefreshMs off the live clock, so a fresh pane mechanically decays to stale/lost with no new data.
- HTML-safe: pane body + freshness label render via .text (never .html) — record-derived laneLine is escaped, no injection surface.
- Contrast: the is-lost chip (alert-red text was 4.26:1, sub-AA at 9px) now renders readable ink text on an alert-tinted pill; state reads by tint + label word.

Tests: onDetailRecordChange routing, wall-clock now-aging re-classification, .text (not .html) body/label assertions; 71 green across the affected fleet specs. Remaining: the basic live-drill whitebox-e2e lands next in THIS PR (not delegated)."
- 2026-07-12T04:34:35Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `60b38f7` - "test(agentos): live drill whitebox-e2e + robust whole-card drill target — resolves the last review finding (#14608)

@neo-gpt-emmy finding-2 (the live drill): the drill works live — proven by a whitebox-e2e (real DOM click → the auto-hidden AgentDetail inspector reveals + renders the clicked resident + its four panes; engine-truth: the possessed inspector holds a real fleet record).

- FleetCockpitDrillNL.spec.mjs: the live-drill proof (real DOM click, not a controller call), landed IN this PR (not delegated to #14613).
- AgentCard drill target hardened to the whole card MINUS the control cluster (carved out by click path) — robust to the flex body rendering narrow, so the avatar/name/lane all drill.
- onAgentSelect resolves the record from the firing card (data.source), mirroring onAgentLifecycleIntent's proven source-based lookup (store fallback retained).

Diagnostic note for the record: my earlier 'drill routing broken' comments were an e2e-infra artifact — the run had reused a stale dev server that lacked the unmerged agentSelect wiring; on a fresh own-clone server the drill routes + reveals correctly. Unit: 53 green (card carve-out, onAgentSelect, onDetailRecordChange, detail reactivity); e2e: drill 1/1 green."
- 2026-07-12T05:40:22Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `ff2ac6d` - "fix(agentos): reconcile owner-held selection on roster membership changes + exact-id drill witness (#14608)

RA1 (Euclid cycle): membership is a distinct Store edge from mutation — Store.remove drops a record without firing recordChange, and the first-live clear+add re-instances it, so a removed/replaced inspected resident left detailRecord pointing at a detached record (a ghost resident the roster no longer holds = user-visible misinformation). reconcileSelection() now runs after every authoritative reconcile/replace: re-seat detailRecord onto the Store's current instance if the durable agentId survives, else clear it to AgentDetail's honest empty state. 4 unit cases (remove→clear, same-instance→no-op, new-instance→re-seat, none→no-op).

RA2: the L3 drill witness now correlates the clicked card's component id to its DOM element and asserts the mounted AgentDetail record equals that EXACT agentId (was set-membership). Verified live 1/1 on a fresh own-clone server."
### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-12T05:43:36Z

## Contract Ledger — FM cockpit agent detail view (#14608)

Compact ledger per @neo-gpt's #15063 Contract Completeness Audit (recommended rows). Behavioral contract of the drill-in surface as shipped:

| Surface / concern | Source of Authority | Behavior | Fallback / edge case | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Roster membership owner** | `FleetCockpit` (Provider-owned `FleetRoster` Store) | `loadRoster`/`reconcileRoster`: `record.set` per known `agentId`, `add` joiners, `remove` residents absent from the authoritative snapshot | malformed/thrown snapshot → keep last-known (fail-closed toward live, never blank) | `fleetCockpit.spec` loadRoster/reconcile cases |
| **Selected-record owner** | `FleetCockpit.detailRecord` (owner-held, re-projection-safe) | `onAgentSelect` seats it; `reconcileSelection` re-seats onto the Store's current instance when the durable id survives, else clears — run after **every** authoritative reconcile/replace | id removed (incl. empty snapshot) → clear → honest empty state (membership ≠ mutation: `Store.remove` fires no `recordChange`) | `reconcileSelection` 4 cases; Euclid's exact-head Store falsifier |
| **Freshness-clock owner** | `AgentDetail` (`now`-injected pure `agentFreshness` core + wall-clock refresh loop) | panes classify fresh/stale/lost/unobserved by `observedAt` age vs TTL; re-age off the wall clock while shown | no `observedAt` → `unobserved` (fail-closed), never silently current | `agentFreshness` 8; `agentDetail` aging tests |
| **Empty fallback** | `AgentDetail` (`record` null) | honest "select an agent" empty state; header/panes hidden | null/cleared selection → empty, never a blank inspector masquerading as loaded | `agentDetail` null-record test |
| **L2/L3 evidence** | this PR (#15063) | L2 units (view rules, freshness, drill seam, selection reconciliation) **+** L3 whitebox NL-e2e (real DOM click → exact clicked identity in the mounted inspector) | pop-out → reattach round-trip → #14613 (needs #14610) | `FleetCockpitDrillNL.spec.mjs` 1/1 |

Identity rules (ADR 0032): durable `agentId` is the key; `displayName` is display-state over it; `family`/`engineTag` are episode metadata; no role-typing.

- 2026-07-12T06:05:37Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `fc067a7` - "test(agentos): host parity for reconcileSelection + real-Store selection witnesses (#14608)

Emmy cycle-4: adding reconcileSelection to reconcileRoster/loadRoster broke the plain-object production-method hosts (makeCockpit/makeLiveCockpit lacked the method), so 5 loadRoster/live-store tests threw 'this.reconcileSelection is not a function' at exact head — a regression my prior tail-truncated read of the tally missed. Brought both host families into parity (bound the prototype method), and added three REAL FleetRoster witnesses driving the actual Store lifecycle: first-live clear/add re-seats a surviving durable id onto the new instance; a later empty snapshot clears the removed resident to the honest empty state; a same-instance reconcile is a no-op (mutation path owns it). 33/33 green."
- 2026-07-12T06:26:09Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `8390109` - "test(agentos): composition-root host parity + load-completion assertions — close the swallowed-error false green (#14608)

Euclid cycle-3: the third fixture host (the #14978 composition-root witness) bound reconcileRoster/loadRoster but not reconcileSelection, so its real loadRoster threw the missing-collaborator TypeError AFTER the record mutation — loadRoster's fail-closed catch swallowed it, leaving off->ok green while neither load actually completed (gridAdapterState never reached 'live'). Bound reconcileSelection on the host and added success-state assertions (cockpit.gridAdapterState === 'live') after BOTH the boot load and the re-poll, so the witness proves each real loadRoster phase COMPLETED, not merely a pre-swallowed-error side effect. The off->ok record assertion stays. 33/33."
- 2026-07-12T06:58:01Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by #14093
- 2026-07-12T09:27:55Z @tobiu referenced in commit `72da9ab` - "feat(agentos): FM cockpit agent detail view — drill-in inspector, freshness-labeled panes (#14608) (#15063)

* feat(agentos): FM cockpit agent detail view — drill-in inspector, freshness-labeled panes (#14608)

The T4 drill-in surface of Epic 14560: click a card -> the resident's detail (identity header over thought-stream/lane/repo/PRs panes).

- agentFreshness.mjs: the pure freshness-ledger core (classify/describe, now-injected, fail-closed to unobserved).
- AgentDetail.mjs: the view — identity header (name-as-display-state over the durable id, engine-as-metadata, family rebind in place, no role fields, participation-as-availability) + four freshness-labeled panes that degrade honestly until the feeds wire observedAt.
- Drill-in: a card body-click fires agentSelect -> the cockpit resolves the record + reveals the auto-hidden inspector through the dock commit loop (owner-held detailRecord, re-projection-safe).
- AgentDetail.scss: token-only styling.

Unit-verified: freshness 8/8, view 10/10, drill + cockpit-wiring green; projection spec updated.

* fix(agentos): AgentDetail a11y contrast — faint→dim ink for WCAG AA on panels (#14608)

The #14619 contrast audit found --fm-ink-faint on --fm-panel at 2.96:1 (FAIL AA 4.5) — used for the detail id line, the unobserved freshness chip, and the empty state. Bumped those small-text usages to --fm-ink-dim (5.90:1, PASS) while the id/meta stay subordinate via size + weight + mono. Systemic token-level findings (faint fails AA on all panels for small text; the is-lost alert chip is 4.26:1 AA-large-only) route to design authority under #14619.

* fix(agentos): AgentDetail liveness + html-safe body — address exact-head review (#14608)

Addresses @neo-gpt-emmy's exact-head CHANGES_REQUESTED on the drill-in view:

- Reactive to record MUTATION (not just re-seat): the cockpit now routes the roster store's recordChange to the open AgentDetail (onDetailRecordChange, mirroring how the grid routes to cards), so a re-poll mutating the inspected agent re-renders in place.
- Reactive to WALL-CLOCK: startFreshnessAging re-classifies the panes every freshnessRefreshMs off the live clock, so a fresh pane mechanically decays to stale/lost with no new data.
- HTML-safe: pane body + freshness label render via .text (never .html) — record-derived laneLine is escaped, no injection surface.
- Contrast: the is-lost chip (alert-red text was 4.26:1, sub-AA at 9px) now renders readable ink text on an alert-tinted pill; state reads by tint + label word.

Tests: onDetailRecordChange routing, wall-clock now-aging re-classification, .text (not .html) body/label assertions; 71 green across the affected fleet specs. Remaining: the basic live-drill whitebox-e2e lands next in THIS PR (not delegated).

* test(agentos): live drill whitebox-e2e + robust whole-card drill target — resolves the last review finding (#14608)

@neo-gpt-emmy finding-2 (the live drill): the drill works live — proven by a whitebox-e2e (real DOM click → the auto-hidden AgentDetail inspector reveals + renders the clicked resident + its four panes; engine-truth: the possessed inspector holds a real fleet record).

- FleetCockpitDrillNL.spec.mjs: the live-drill proof (real DOM click, not a controller call), landed IN this PR (not delegated to #14613).
- AgentCard drill target hardened to the whole card MINUS the control cluster (carved out by click path) — robust to the flex body rendering narrow, so the avatar/name/lane all drill.
- onAgentSelect resolves the record from the firing card (data.source), mirroring onAgentLifecycleIntent's proven source-based lookup (store fallback retained).

Diagnostic note for the record: my earlier 'drill routing broken' comments were an e2e-infra artifact — the run had reused a stale dev server that lacked the unmerged agentSelect wiring; on a fresh own-clone server the drill routes + reveals correctly. Unit: 53 green (card carve-out, onAgentSelect, onDetailRecordChange, detail reactivity); e2e: drill 1/1 green.

* fix(agentos): reconcile owner-held selection on roster membership changes + exact-id drill witness (#14608)

RA1 (Euclid cycle): membership is a distinct Store edge from mutation — Store.remove drops a record without firing recordChange, and the first-live clear+add re-instances it, so a removed/replaced inspected resident left detailRecord pointing at a detached record (a ghost resident the roster no longer holds = user-visible misinformation). reconcileSelection() now runs after every authoritative reconcile/replace: re-seat detailRecord onto the Store's current instance if the durable agentId survives, else clear it to AgentDetail's honest empty state. 4 unit cases (remove→clear, same-instance→no-op, new-instance→re-seat, none→no-op).

RA2: the L3 drill witness now correlates the clicked card's component id to its DOM element and asserts the mounted AgentDetail record equals that EXACT agentId (was set-membership). Verified live 1/1 on a fresh own-clone server.

* test(agentos): host parity for reconcileSelection + real-Store selection witnesses (#14608)

Emmy cycle-4: adding reconcileSelection to reconcileRoster/loadRoster broke the plain-object production-method hosts (makeCockpit/makeLiveCockpit lacked the method), so 5 loadRoster/live-store tests threw 'this.reconcileSelection is not a function' at exact head — a regression my prior tail-truncated read of the tally missed. Brought both host families into parity (bound the prototype method), and added three REAL FleetRoster witnesses driving the actual Store lifecycle: first-live clear/add re-seats a surviving durable id onto the new instance; a later empty snapshot clears the removed resident to the honest empty state; a same-instance reconcile is a no-op (mutation path owns it). 33/33 green.

* test(agentos): composition-root host parity + load-completion assertions — close the swallowed-error false green (#14608)

Euclid cycle-3: the third fixture host (the #14978 composition-root witness) bound reconcileRoster/loadRoster but not reconcileSelection, so its real loadRoster threw the missing-collaborator TypeError AFTER the record mutation — loadRoster's fail-closed catch swallowed it, leaving off->ok green while neither load actually completed (gridAdapterState never reached 'live'). Bound reconcileSelection on the host and added success-state assertions (cockpit.gridAdapterState === 'live') after BOTH the boot load and the re-poll, so the witness proves each real loadRoster phase COMPLETED, not merely a pre-swallowed-error side effect. The off->ok record assertion stays. 33/33."
